i reviewed our QC notes. i would exclude the following markers from all analyses: H3.1, pRPA32, the first Ki67, TNFR1, PCNA, and EGFR. i would also recommend excluding ER, PR, HER2 because we're working with TNBCs. the TMA collection included tumors positive for these markers, but we haven't been working with them. let me know if you need further clarification on this!
also, once you have a UTAG result that seems good, it would be helpful to include some visualization of the spatial distribution within some of the cores to get a sense of how much mixing there is of them, how many might occur in a given core, etc. thank you for all of your efforts!

Results from Inga-Maria

Neighborhoods were computed with r=50 and spatial kmeans k=25

This results based on the celltypes, so we can see that several clusters have no tumor celltype. 
But in my result figure, it does not look like this. The RPDs are mixed by tumor and other celltypes.

RCN
Recurrent Cellular Neighborhood

Refers to clusters of spatially organized cells that are defined by their cell types (more stable classifications based on cell lineage and differentiation)

By mapping spatial positions and cell types within the tissue, RCN analysis quantifies the interactions and co-localizations of different cell types within a defined radius, capturing recurring spatial patterns of cell identity.

RPD
Recurrent Phenotyping Domain

Refers to clusters of spatially organized cells that share specific phenotypes (distinct expression profiles based on marker expression, indicating functional or activation states)

By mapping spatial positions and marker expression within the tissue, RPD analysis quantifies the interactions and co-localizations of different phenotypes within a defined radius, highlighting consistent functional or state-based patterns.

Differences between RCN and RPD

Differences between methods

Scimap

Based on location and cell types.

The main focus is on the classification of cells and their spatial distribution relationship.

Problems that tend to be solved:
Spatial relationships between cell populations: Interaction patterns of different cell types, such as whether immune cells tend to gather around certain specific types of cancer cells.

Functional connections between tissue structure and cell types.

To solve different problems

UTAG

Based on location and marker expression.

Instead of relying on a priori cell classification, it’s performed directly based on the protein marker expression pattern of each cell.

Problems that tend to be solved:
Spatial relationships of cell states: the activation or inhibition in th tumor microenvironment.

Identify microenvironment features: whether a microenvironment with high expression of certain proteins is more inclined to attract specific cell types.

Which types of cells are near each other, and whether these relationships are related to certain functions or pathologies.

How do expression patterns vary between cells, and how do changes reflect underlying biological functions.

The distribution of marker expression values

The data is concentrated near a peak value of 0.4.
A clear right-skewed distribution, the long tail is on the right.

Use median instead of mean.
Use z-score to normalize each marker.

z-score

z-scores are calculated per row

Results of UTAG

Set a threshold of hierarchical tree
Combine 98 UTAG clusters to 23 RPDs based on the marker expression
Column name is RPDs

Functional marker expression (Median) in UTAG clusters (98)

Set a threshold of hierarchical tree
Combine 23 RPDs to 10 new RPDs based on the marker expression within RPDs
Column name is RPDs_2

Functional marker expression (Median) in RPDs (23)

Stacked Bar Plot of NQO1_GCLC_VIM by RPDs_2 (Percentage)

RPD 4, 5, 9 have relatively high proportion of NQO1+

RPD 2, 3 have relatively high proportion of GCLC+Vim+

RPD 1, 7 have relatively high proportion of GCLC+Vim-

RPD 8 has relatively high proportion of GCLC-Vim+

“NQO1 had a more even expression across neighborhoods, including in tumor cell-rich neighborhoods”

NQO1 was especially high in tumor cell-rich neighborhoods (RPD 4,5,9)

GCLC was especially high in CD4 T cell enriched neighborhood (RPD 1,2,3,7)

Different immnue cells showed a higher density in the vicinity of GCLC+VIM+ (RPD2,3) compared to GCLC-VIM+ (RPD 8) or GCLC+VIM- (RPD 1,7)

GCLC+VIM+ cells have a strong association with immune cell populations

RPD6, a small beighborhood, we can check it on images larter.

Red: NQO1+

Purple: GCLC+VIM+

Brown: GCLC+VIM-

Pink: GCLC-VIM+

Stacked Bar Plot of Cell types by RPDs (Percentage)

All cells

Only tumor

After only tumor cells remain, the proportion of GCLC+Vim+ decreased significantly (RPD 2 and 4), but the NQO1_GCLC_VIM composition of each RPDs was roughly the same.

Next step

Check the proportion of patients in each RPD to confirm whether there is a sampling effect.

Try to use all markers to run UTAG, and to compare the differences.

Compare the differences between results with all cells and only cancer cells.
